§6Bedrock Ore Processing§f

§aA comprehensive system for infinite resource extraction — centrifuge, wash, roast, and process bedrock ores for maximum yields!§f

Bedrock Ores appear in the bedrock layer under depth ore veins and provide infinite resources when properly processed. Each vein has a specific composition that determines what ores it yields. The processing chain involves multiple stages, each adding byproducts and increasing yields.

§6Processing Stages§f:
§b1. Extraction§f: Large Mining Drill extracts raw bedrock ore (needs drillbit + power, ~5 min per ore).
§b2. Slopping§f: Bedrock Ore Processor splits raw ore into 6 categories (Light Metal, Heavy Metal, Rare Earth, Actinide, Non-Metal, Crystalline).
§b3. Centrifuging§f: First centrifuge pass produces a second-stage ore that can be centrifuged again or washed.
§b4. Washing§f (Ore Acidizer):
  §b–§f Water → duplicates all products.
  §b–§f Sulfuric Acid → adds tier 1 byproducts.
  §b–§f Solvent → adds tier 2 byproducts.
  §b–§f High-Performance Solvent → adds tier 3 byproducts (also yields lower tiers).
  §b–§f Liquid Hydrogen → increases primary product yield.
  §b–§f Chlorine Gas → increases secondary product yield.
§b5. Roasting§f (Combination/Pyrolysis Oven): Produces Vitriol as bonus byproduct without affecting ore output.

§6Processing Diagram§f: Raw → Centrifuged → Cleaned → Separated → [Purified / Nitrated → Nitrocrystalline → Deep Cleaned / Seared]. Enriched stage can be done at any time for final products.

§eTip§f: All ore types share the exact same processing chain — you can route all six categories through the same machines. Never skip the §bwater wash§f step (doubles all products for free)!
